body: PT Bukit Asam Tbk has signed an MoU with three state-owned banks to optimize the allocation of DHE SDA (Domestic Hedging Exchange and Settlement of Export Proceeds) funds, aiming to boost the company's contribution to the national economy. As of June 2024, the DHE SDA placement reached IDR 1.6 trillion (USD 95.8 million), allocated to domestic financial instruments in Bank Indonesia and other partner banks.
title: PT Bukit Asam signs MoU with three state-owned banks to optimize forex export proceeds from natural resource exports.

body: The Indonesian government's latest auction of government securities received IDR 46.65 trillion in incoming bids, with a total awarded amount of IDR 24 trillion from 7 series of state treasury notes and government bonds. Since the beginning of 2024, the government has conducted 18 SUN auctions, accumulating IDR 1,031.69 trillion in bids and IDR 415.61 trillion in awarded amounts.
title: Indonesian Government Raises IDR 24 Trillion from Latest Auction of Government Securities

body: PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama Tbk has secured a USD 1 million loan from LX International Corp, an affiliate, at a 6.20% annual interest rate, which is lower than the bank rate, to support its nickel exploration business operations. The loan is considered more efficient than a bank loan, and the company's shares are valued at IDR 60.82 billion, with major shareholders including EBI, PT Sungai Mas Minerals, and PT Inti Mega Ventura.
title: PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama receives USD 1 million loan from affiliate LX International Corp to support nickel exploration operations.

body: PT Verona Indah Pictures Tbk and PT Master Print Tbk are conducting an initial public offering (IPO) process, with fixed prices set at IDR 195 and IDR 128 per share, respectively, aiming to raise IDR 218.72 billion and IDR 55.68 billion in funds. This brings the total funds raised from IPOs on the Indonesia Stock Exchange to IDR 5.15 trillion, with 34 companies listed since the beginning of the year and 30 more in the pipeline.
title: PT Verona Indah Pictures and PT Master Print Conduct Initial Public Offerings on Indonesia Stock Exchange

body: PT Master Print Tbk is acquiring 99% of PT Global Putra Kusuma, a distributor of consumables for small and medium enterprises, for IDR 25.09 billion, expanding its market share in the SME B2C segment. The acquired company, PT Global Putra Kusuma, reported strong Q1 2024 performance, with sales up 64% to IDR 5.50 billion, gross profit up 84% to IDR 2.78 billion, and net profit doubling to IDR 1.05 billion.
title: PT Master Print to Acquire 99% of PT Global Putra Kusuma, Expanding Market Share in SME B2C Segment.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) surged to 7,626.64 at the closing bell, up from its opening position of 7,559.27, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL also rising by 1.54%, 1.61%, 1.38%, and 1.26%, respectively. Blue-chip stocks including PT Bank Central Asia, PT Telkom Indonesia, and PT Indofood Sukses Makmur benefited from the positive trend, while others like PT Bukit Asam and PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia declined.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Surges to 7,626.64 with Most Indices and Blue-Chip Stocks Rising

body: PT Prima Globalindo Logistik Tbk, a logistics delivery company, plans to distribute an interim dividend of IDR 1.54 billion, or IDR 2 per share, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners on September 27, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 5.27 billion in the first half of 2024, with retained earnings of IDR 39.95 billion and total equity of IDR 148.12 billion as of June 30, 2024.
title: PT Prima Globalindo Logistik to Distribute IDR 1.54 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: BUMA's bond issuance of IDR 1 trillion was oversubscribed 1.4 times, with coupons ranging from 7.25% to 9.75% per annum and tenors from 370 days to 5 years, indicating high demand from investors. The proceeds will be used for various purposes, including paying off series Rupiah Bond BUMA I 2023, capital expenditure for heavy equipment acquisition, and working capital support for operational activities.
title: BUMA's 2024 Bond Issue Oversubscribed 1.4 Times, Raising IDR 1 Trillion in Principal.

body: PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk (SMMA) has made a capital investment of IDR 95 billion in PT Liugong Finance Indonesia, acquiring a 19% stake in the company. This investment does not exceed 20% of Liugong Finance's total equity, making it a non-material transaction, according to SMMA's President Director Burhanuddin Abdullah.
title: Sinar Mas Multiartha Invests IDR 95 Billion in Liugong Finance Indonesia

body: PT Petrosea TBK has allocated IDR 6 trillion for capital expenditure to acquire new equipment, supporting business expansion in Central Kalimantan, with the first-phase investment allocated to several suppliers. The company has also initiated operations in new mining projects, including PT Multi Tambangjaya Utama's project in South Biro Regency and FCO Life-of-Mine project in Kapuas Regency, both starting in September 2024.
title: Petrosea Allocates IDR 6 Trillion for New Equipment to Support Business Expansion in Central Kalimantan.

body: PT Pertamina Geothermal Energi (PGeo) has signed an MoU with Genvia, a French clean hydrogen technology company, to jointly research and develop low-carbon hydrogen using geothermal energy, aiming to create an economic model for a competitive green hydrogen business. This partnership is expected to strengthen cross-border collaboration and advance the development and adoption of green energy, with PGeo and Genvia working together to enter the global green energy market.
title: Pertamina Geothermal Energy signs MOU with Genvia to develop clean hydrogen technology.

body: PT Garuda Indonesia's loss worsened by 32.88% year-on-year to USD 101.65 million in H1 2024, despite an 18.24% revenue growth to USD 1.62 billion, driven by increased revenue from scheduled flights, unscheduled flights, and other services. However, operating expenses surged 23.34% year-on-year to USD 1.53 billion, led by flight operation expenses, repair maintenance, and other costs, offsetting the revenue growth and contributing to the net loss.
title: Garuda Indonesia's loss widens to $101.65 million in H1 2024 due to increased operating expenses despite revenue growth.

body: PT Indika Energy Tbk has divested its 6,332 shares in PT Trisetia Citagraha to PT Barito Pacific Lumber for IDR 26.77 billion, with IDR 22.29 billion paid in the first stage and the remaining amount to be paid in the next stage. The divestment, aligned with Indika's business diversification strategy, will result in PT Trisetia Citagraha no longer being a subsidiary company and will not be consolidated into Indika's financial reports.
title: Indika Energy divests subsidiary PT Trisetia Citagraha to Barito Pacific Lumber for IDR 26.77 billion.

body: PT BPD Jawa Timur TBK's shareholders approved a capital investment of IDR 10 billion in PT BPD Banten TBK, with 98.219% of the meeting quorum and 82.276% of total issued shares supporting the corporate action. As of August 2024, PT BPD Jawa Timur TBK has 15.01 billion shares, with controllers owning 51.12% and non-controlling shareholders holding 48.73%, while PT BPD Banten TBK has 51.87 billion shares, with controllers owning 66.11% and non-controlling shareholders holding 33.99%.
title: PT BPD Jawa Timur shareholders approve IDR 10 billion capital investment in PT BPD Banten.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) fell 0.75% to 7,592.64 at the morning session close, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESG also slipping into the red zone. Several stocks, including PT Chandra Asri Pacific TBK and PT Bank Central Asia TBK, were corrected, while others like PT Bayan Resources TBK and PT Petrosea TBK saw gains.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Falls 75 Points to 7,590 in Morning Session

body: PT Dima Investindo has acquired 55% of PT Miki Ojisan Indomitra's shares in PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa Tbk (KMDs) for IDR 184.8 billion, resulting in a change of controlling shareholder and triggering a mandatory tender offer for KMDs' shares. The acquisition price was IDR 420 per share, lower than KMDs' closing price of IDR 725 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 580 billion and 800 million listed shares.
title: PT Dima Investindo acquires 55% stake in PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa for IDR 184.8 billion.

body: Foreign investors bought a net 426.93 million shares on Friday, September 27, with significant purchases in digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, domestic shipping services PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk, and real estate development PT Wulandari Bangun Laksana Tbk. Meanwhile, financial stocks dominated the top sell list, with notable sales in PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk, PT Bank Mandiri Tbk, and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk.
title: Foreign investors net buy 426.93 million shares in Indonesian market on Friday.

body: UOB Kay Hian Private Limited, a Singaporean investor, increased its stake in PT Centratama Telekomunikasi Indonesia TBK to 96.17% by purchasing 18.04 billion shares, becoming the controlling shareholder. Meanwhile, other foreign investors, including DBS Bank Ltd and CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd, made notable transactions, acquiring shares in companies such as PT Lippo Karawaci TBK, PT Paperocks Indonesia TBK, and PT Wilton Makmur Indonesia TBK.
title: Foreign investors acquire significant stakes in several Indonesian companies, including Centratama Telekomunikasi and Lippo Karawaci.

body: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara TBK reported a net profit of IDR 1.60 trillion in H1 2024, a 9.40% year-on-year increase driven by revenue growth, with total revenue reaching IDR 6.15 trillion, up 6.52% from H1 2023. The company's gross profit also rose to IDR 4.29 trillion, with major contributors to revenue including PT Indosat TBK, PT XL Axiata TBK, and PT Telekomunikasi Seluler.
title: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara TBK Sees 9.4% Increase in Net Profit in H1 2024 Due to Revenue Growth.

body: PT United Tractors Tbk, a subsidiary of PT Astra International Tbk, will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 2.42 trillion, or IDR 667 per share, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners. The company reported a net profit of IDR 9.53 trillion as of June 30, 2024, and has seen its stock climb 2.12% in the past 5 days and 18% since the beginning of the year.
title: PT United Tractors to Distribute IDR 2.42 Trillion Interim Dividend to Shareholders
